request.question: Aqar Development: why did we decline it last year, and does anything in the latest macro data change that call?

events.text:
## Recommendation

The decline should stand. Nothing in the latest macro data changes the call, because the dec
line rested on a mandate test, not a market view, and macro developments do not override

that test.

## Rationale

**Why Aqar was declined.** The opportunity was a
Saudi real estate developer active in residential and mixed-use projects.
Aqar Development is a privately held
Saudi real estate developer active in residential and mixed-use projects in Riyadh and the Eastern Province, reaching Lunar as an in
bound introduction in August 2025, seeking growth capital of SAR 38 million in a Series B round.

events.quote:
Opportunity summary
Aqar Development is a privately held Saudi real estate developer active in residential and mixed-use projects
in Riyadh and the Eastern Province. The opportunity reached Lunar as

events.text: The screen failed on sector alone.
The sector test is
dispositive: real estate development falls outside the Technology & Consumer sector mandate, and under the Charter a sector failure ends the screening process.

events.quote:
Mandate test Charter requirement Aqar Development Result
Sector Technology & Consumer Real estate development Fail
Ticket size SAR 20-60M per deal SAR 38M sought Within band (moot)
Stage Series A t

events.text: Notably, the other two tests would have passed:
ticket size (SAR 38M sought) was within the SAR 20-60M band, and the Series B stage was within the Series A to
Series C window, both moot given the sector fail.

events.quote:
Mandate test Charter requirement Aqar Development Result
Sector Technology & Consumer Real estate development Fail
Ticket size SAR 20-60M per deal SAR 38M sought Within band (moot)
Stage Series A t

events.text: This aligns with the Charter, which is explicit that sector is a hard g
ate.
Real estate, industrials, energy, and healthcare delivery are outside the mandate and are decl
ined at screening regardless of financial merit.

events.quote:
Real estate, industrials, energy, and healthcare delivery are outside the mandate and are declined at screening regardless of
financial merit.

events.text: Because the sector test failed,
no valuation work, financial anal
ysis, quantified risk assessment, or compliance screen was performed, and none should be inferred from the memorandum.

events.quote:
Accordingly, no valuation work,
financial analysis, quantified risk assessment, or compliance screen was performed, and none should be
inferred from this memorandum.

events.text: **Why macro data does not change it
.** The decline memo pre-empts exactly this question.
This decline is a mandate determ
ination. It is not a view on the quality of Aqar Development, its management, or the outlook for the Saudi real estate sector, and it does not carry a val

uation opinion of any kind. The determination can change only if the Committee amends the sector mandate in the Charter by formal resolution. Market or macroeconomic developments, however favorable to the sector, do not
override the sector test and are not grounds for re-screening while the current mandate stands.

events.quote:
Re-evaluation provision
This decline is a mandate determination. It is not a view on the quality of Aqar Development, its
management, or the outlook for the Saudi real estate sector, and it does not

events.text: For completeness, the recent macro backdrop is not even direct
ional support for a reopening:
real GDP grew 3
.0% year-on-year in Q1 2026

events.quote:
Real GDP Growth
Gross Domestic Product (GDP) Publication for Q1 of 2026
GASTAT · 9 June 2026 · stats.gov.sa/en/w/news/199
Real GDP grew 3.0% year-on-year in Q1 2026, driven by broadly even growth a

events.text: , and while construction-adjacent housing
costs are rising, that shows in CPI as a cost line, not a mandate signal:
the CPI recorded an annual increase of 1.8% in May 2026, driven mainly by housing, water,
electricity, gas & other fuels (+3.7%).

events.quote:
Inflation — Consumer & Wholesale Prices
Consumer Price Index – May 2026
GASTAT · 14 June 2026 · stats.gov.sa/documents/d/guest/cpi-may-2026-en-pdf
The CPI recorded an annual increase of 1.8% in May

events.text: None of these figures spe
ak to Technology & Consumer, which is where the mandate confines new comm

itments.

## What would change the conclusion

The single sensitivity is a Charter amendment, not a data point.
The determination can change only if the Committee amends the sector mandate in the Charter by formal resolution; if the mandate is amended, any renewed interest in the company would enter the stand
ard process from the beginning: screening, then analysis, then Committee review.

events.quote:
The determination can change only if the Committee amends the sector mandate in the Charter by
formal resolution.

If the mandate is
amended, any renewed interest in the company would enter the standard process from the beginning:
screening, then analysis, then Committee review.

events.text: Judgment: the one scenario that could bring Aqar back inside the mandate without
a Charter change is a factual reclassification, i.e. if the company were shown to have a material technology or
consumer-platform component rather than pure property development. The screening desk explicitly checked this and found none.
The scre

ening desk verified with the introducer that the company's revenue derives from property development and sales, with no material technology or consumer-platform component that could bring the business within the mand
ate definition.

events.quote:
The screening desk verified with the introducer that the company's revenue derives from property
development and sales, with no material technology or consumer-platform component that could bring the

events.text: Evidence that would revisit that: a substantiated revenue breakdown showing a

genuine retail-technology or consumer-platform business line. Absent that, the decline holds.

Advis
ory only: the investment decision rests with the committee.
